questions.question: What does a political party aim to achieve in a democracy?
questions.answer: To gain political power and implement policies.

questions.question: Which system allows only one political party to rule a country?
Which system is dominated by two major political parties?
Which system allows several parties to compete for power?
Why do coalition governments form?
questions.answer: Because no single party wins a majority.

questions.question: What problem occurs when power is concentrated among a few party leaders?
What challenge occurs when leadership remains within families?
Which factor influences elections through financial resources?
What does the anti-defection law control?
Which factor ensures voters know the background of candidates?
What role do political parties play in the legislature?
questions.answer: They guide members in making laws.

questions.question: How do political parties influence government policies?
questions.answer: By implementing their ideology and election promises.

questions.question: Why are regional parties important in coalition politics?
questions.answer: They can influence national government decisions.

questions.question: Which institution supervises elections and political parties in India?
Which feature allows representation of diverse social interests?
What role do opposition parties play in democracy?
questions.answer: They ensure accountability of the government.

questions.question: What type of political parties focus mainly on regional issues?
Which challenge reduces opportunities for new leaders in politics?
Which democratic principle allows peaceful transfer of power?
How do alliances improve election chances?
questions.answer: By combining support from multiple parties.

questions.question: Which factor helps political parties influence public opinion?
Which challenge reduces voters’ meaningful choices?
Which reform increases transparency in candidate information?
What role does media play in democratic politics?
questions.answer: It informs citizens and monitors political activities.

questions.question: Why are political reforms necessary?
questions.answer: To strengthen transparency, accountability, and democracy.
